steps.text: This is a clock! Clocks have numbers all the way around. Can you tap the number 1?
steps.feedback: One! Now tap 2!
Now tap 3!
Now tap 4!
Now tap 5!
Now tap 6 — it's at the very bottom!
Halfway around. Now tap 7!
Now tap 8!
Now tap 9!
Now tap 10!
Now tap 11!
Last one — tap 12 at the very top!
You counted all the way around!

steps.text: Some numbers on the clock have special jobs — they point in every direction. The 12 lives at the very top. Can you tap it?
steps.feedback: 12 at the top — the clock always starts here! Now find the 6 at the bottom.
At the bottom, opposite 12. Now find the 3!
On the right side! Last one — find the 9!
On the left. These four numbers point in every direction!

text: You found all four special numbers!
Amazing — you know your way around!

steps.text: The clock has two hands — one long, one short. They tell us the time! Can you tap the long hand?
steps.feedback: That's the long hand — it counts the minutes. Now tap the short hand!
The short hand tells us the hour — like 1 o'clock or 3 o'clock. You know them both!

text: Long hand, short hand — you know them both!

steps.text: Different times of day have different names. Press Breakfast — what time do you eat breakfast?
steps.feedback: The short hand is near the 10. Now press Lunch!
The short hand has moved to the 1. Now press Dinner!
It's getting dark outside! Now press Bedtime!
Time to sleep. You know your whole day!

text: Breakfast, lunch, dinner, bedtime — you know your whole day!

steps.text: Let's watch the day go by — from the moment you wake up to when the stars come out! Press Morning!
steps.feedback: The sun is just coming up. Now press Noon!
The sun is right at the top of the sky! Now press Afternoon!
The sun starts moving down. Now press Nighttime!
The moon comes out and the stars appear. You followed the whole day!

text: Morning, noon, afternoon, night — you followed the whole day!
